title: Contains Duplicate
description:
Write a function that scans a list of integers and reports whether any value shows up more than once. Return `True` the moment a repeat exists anywhere in the list, and `False` when every entry is unique.

**Example**

```
contains_duplicate([1, 2, 3, 1])
# -> True   (the value 1 shows up twice)

contains_duplicate([1, 2, 3, 4])
# -> False  (all four values are unique)
```
hints: A set remembers what you've already seen.
If len(set(nums)) < len(nums), there was a duplicate.

solutions.name: Set
solutions.explanation: Add elements to a set as you scan; if you ever see one already present, you found a duplicate. Equivalently, compare the size of the set to the list length.
